claims.text: The convolution operator is the fundamental building block of modern convolutional neural networks (CNNs).
claims.source_quote: The convolution operator is the fundamental building block of modern convolutional neural networks (CNNs), owing to its simplicity, translational equivariance, and efficient implementation.

claims.text: The convolution operator possesses translational equivariance.
claims.source_quote: The convolution operator is the fundamental building block of modern convolutional neural networks (CNNs), owing to its simplicity, translational equivariance, and efficient implementation.

claims.text: The convolution operator has an efficient implementation.
claims.source_quote: The convolution operator is the fundamental building block of modern convolutional neural networks (CNNs), owing to its simplicity, translational equivariance, and efficient implementation.

claims.text: The convolution operator is a fixed, linear, locally-averaging operator.
claims.source_quote: its structure as a fixed, linear, locally-averaging operator limits its ability to capture structured signal properties such as low-rank decompositions, adaptive basis representations, and non-uniform spatial dependencies.

claims.text: The convolution operator's structure limits its ability to capture low-rank decompositions.
claims.source_quote: its structure as a fixed, linear, locally-averaging operator limits its ability to capture structured signal properties such as low-rank decompositions, adaptive basis representations, and non-uniform spatial dependencies.

claims.text: The convolution operator's structure limits its ability to capture adaptive basis representations.
claims.source_quote: its structure as a fixed, linear, locally-averaging operator limits its ability to capture structured signal properties such as low-rank decompositions, adaptive basis representations, and non-uniform spatial dependencies.

claims.text: The convolution operator's structure limits its ability to capture non-uniform spatial dependencies.
claims.source_quote: its structure as a fixed, linear, locally-averaging operator limits its ability to capture structured signal properties such as low-rank decompositions, adaptive basis representations, and non-uniform spatial dependencies.

claims.text: The paper organises alternative operators into five families: decomposition-based operators, adaptive weighted operators, basis-adaptive operators, integral and kernel operators, and attention-based operators.
claims.source_quote: We organise the landscape of alternative operators into five families: (i) decomposition-based operators, which separate structural and noise components through singular value or tensor decompositions; (ii) adaptive weighted operators, which modulate kernel contributions as a function of spatial position or signal content; (iii) basis-adaptive operators, which optimise the analysis bases together with the network weights; (iv) integral and kernel operators, which generalise the convolution to position-dependent and non-linear kernels; and (v) attention-based operators, which relax the locality assumption entirely.

claims.text: Decomposition-based operators separate structural and noise components through singular value or tensor decompositions.

claims.source_quote: basis-adaptive operators, which optimise the analysis bases together with the network weights

claims.source_quote: integral and kernel operators, which generalise the convolution to position-dependent and non-linear kernels

claims.text: Attention-based operators relax the locality assumption entirely.
claims.source_quote: attention-based operators, which relax the locality assumption entirely

claims.text: The paper provides a comparative analysis of all five operator families across the dimensions of linearity, locality, equivariance, computational cost, and suitability for image-to-image and image-to-label tasks.
claims.source_quote: We further provide a comparative analysis of all families across relevant dimensions -- linearity, locality, equivariance, computational cost, and suitability for image-to-image and image-to-label tasks
